name: list_dir
description: Enumerate the entries of a directory in the agent's workspace and return a bounded list of name/kind/size tuples. The agent uses ``list_dir`` to discover what files exist before issuing ``read_file``, ``edit_file``, or ``write_file`` calls -- without it the agent is forced to guess paths, which the Phase 1 benchmark ``benchmarks/tasks/test_write_unit_test.py`` (issue #105 evidence) makes explicit by requiring the agent to locate a target module the benchmark author did not name. The implementation MUST use ``os.scandir`` (Python stdlib) for the directory walk; per docs/SECURITY.md threat #3 the only filesystem-introspection primitive allowed for this skill is the stdlib so the audit surface is one import, not a hand-rolled walker with custom symlink-following policy. Symlinks are NOT followed by default (target kind is reported as the ``symlink`` kind); entries beyond ``max_entries`` are dropped and ``truncated`` is set to ``true`` so a single ``list_dir`` call cannot blow the local-model context window (PRD §4, PHILOSOPHY.md §5, SECURITY.md threat #5). Hidden entries (dotfiles) are excluded unless ``include_hidden`` is set, mirroring the conservative default of ``ls`` so the agent does not silently read ``.env`` or ``.git`` into its context.

path.description: Absolute or harness-relative path to the directory to list. The harness MUST resolve the path against the workspace root and reject any resolution that escapes it (``..`` segments, absolute paths outside the workspace) before the underlying ``os.scandir`` call, mirroring the read_file bounding pattern (issue #20). The directory MUST exist and be readable; a missing or unreadable directory surfaces as ``entries=[]`` and ``truncated=false`` (the agent branches on the absence of expected entries, not on an exception).

glob.description: Optional filename filter applied with ``fnmatch.fnmatch`` (Python stdlib). When set, only entries whose ``name`` matches the pattern are returned. Use ``grep_search`` to filter by file *contents*; this is purely a filename filter so the two skills compose without overlap.

include_hidden.description: When ``true``, entries whose name starts with ``.`` are returned alongside the rest. Defaults to ``false`` so a casual ``list_dir`` cannot leak ``.env``, ``.git``, or editor swap files into the agent's context. Setting this to ``true`` is an explicit opt-in; the agent SHOULD log the call so the trace shows the dotfile exposure (issue #105 evidence).

max_entries.description: Maximum number of entries returned. If the directory contains more entries, the excess are dropped deterministically (sorted by ``name`` ascending) and ``truncated`` is set to ``true``. Defaults to 1000 so a single ``list_dir`` call cannot blow the local-model context window (PRD §4).

entries.description: Sorted (by ``name`` ascending) list of directory entries. Each entry has the trio ``{name, kind, size}`` -- ``name`` is the basename, ``kind`` is one of ``"file"`` / ``"dir"`` / ``"symlink"`` / ``"other"`` (mirroring the ``entry.is_file()`` / ``is_dir()`` / ``is_symlink()`` stdlib predicates; ``"other"`` covers sockets, FIFOs, block devices), and ``size`` is the byte count from ``stat().st_size`` (``0`` for non-regular entries). The list is bounded by ``max_entries``; when it is capped ``truncated`` is ``true`` and the agent MUST re-call with a tighter ``glob`` to narrow the result before paging further.

name.description: Basename of the entry (no directory component). UTF-8; not normalised -- callers compare byte-for-byte. Empty or whitespace-only names are reported as-is so a future script can investigate pathological filesystems.

kind.description: Type tag for the entry, derived from the stdlib ``os.DirEntry`` predicates in this order: ``is_symlink`` -> ``"symlink"`` (even when the link target is a regular file, so the agent can decide whether to follow); ``is_dir`` -> ``"dir"``; ``is_file`` -> ``"file"``; else ``"other"`` (sockets, FIFOs, block devices, character devices).

size.description: Entry size in bytes, read from ``stat().st_size``. ``0`` when ``kind != "file"`` because directory, symlink, and ``other`` sizes are not portable across filesystems (a directory's ``st_size`` is the entry's payload size on some platforms and ``0`` on others; pinning to ``0`` here keeps the contract stable).

truncated.description: ``true`` iff the underlying directory held more entries than ``max_entries`` and the excess were dropped. The agent MUST branch on this value: re-call with a narrower ``glob``, raise ``max_entries``, or move on -- never ignore a ``true`` value, because the missing entries may be exactly the one the agent needs.

truncation_policy.rationale: 1000 entries is ~30 KiB worst case (each entry averages 30 bytes for ``name, kind, size``), which keeps a single ``list_dir`` call well below the 4K-32K context window of typical local 7B-70B models (PRD §4) and bounds a runaway walk so it cannot exhaust context (SECURITY.md threat #5). Phase 3 will add historical-log pruning; this per-call safety bound is the Phase 1 minimum.
truncation_policy.behavior_when_truncated: Sort the full entry set by ``name`` ascending (deterministic, byte-sort via ``sorted()``), emit the first ``max_entries`` items, drop the rest, and set ``truncated=true``. When ``glob`` is the binding filter, the glob is applied first so the cap is applied to the filtered set -- the agent that asks for ``*.py`` never silently drops Python files in favor of, say, ``.pyc`` files.
